## AbstractModelClass.openCursor() method

Opens a iterable cursor, with the cursor object it's possible to iterate one document after another

**Signature:**

```typescript
static openCursor(filter?: QueryRootFilter, options?: QueryOpenCursorOptions): Query<any, any>;
```

## Parameters

<table><thead><tr><th>

Parameter


</th><th>

Type


</th><th>

Description


</th></tr></thead>
<tbody><tr><td>

filter


</td><td>

QueryRootFilter


</td><td>

_(Optional)_


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

options


</td><td>

QueryOpenCursorOptions


</td><td>

_(Optional)_ Query options


</td></tr>
</tbody></table>

**Returns:**

Query&lt;any, any&gt;


## Remarks

Any opeation that takes time in between the iteration of the cursor will result in an error, because the transaction of the cursor will be closed. Be cautious when using the cursor to not do any long operation in between the iteration, if you need to do it, consider using `find` method instead of `openCursor` and iterate the result array.

## Example


```ts
const itr = await UserModel.openCursor({ age: { $gt: 18 } });

for await (const doc of itr) {
  console.log(doc);
}
```
